title: Monch the Lizard and the Minty Meditation
summary: While wandering through a mucky, peaceful swamp in their galoshes, the Mischief Moles encounter a towering, green-scaled lizard creature named Monch. Though initially cautious of his long sticky tongue, the moles help Monch forage for fresh mint to brew a pot of fragrant tea. As they sit by a small fire, the lizard man teaches the moles the calming practice of meditation, guiding them through reflections on the warmth of family love and the simple joy of sharing a collective giggle.

highlightQuote.text: You can't stick... you can't put your sticky tongue on the Mischief Moles. You can't do that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Give me a nut snack. So, this I have to keep out because he eats all of these nuts.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who does?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The mouse. I see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, so the story starts.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Alright, and now we've got stars. We just need stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do a Mischief Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. We're all tucked in with the toys. They're all done. Okay, now come on over with Dada. Hop hop. Now, once upon a time, the Mischief Moles were wandering through a different part of the forest than usual. It was a very wet and mucky part of the forest called the swamp.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep on going and find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And around the corner of the tree poked the head of a creature they'd never seen before. But it was a friendly creature and it said, 'Oh, hello little moles. Welcome to the swamp. What are your names?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Disco and Disco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hello Disco and Disco. My name is... what was this creature's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Snomp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Snomp. I am a lizard folk creature. And that's because it was a creature who was as big as a person but covered with green scales just like a lizard. And he had a pointy face and sharp teeth and a long tongue just like a lizard, and big yellow eyes and a long tail.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, can you please not... can you please not stick on the Mischief Moles?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stick on the Mischief Moles?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can't stick... you can't put your sticky tongue on the Mischief Moles. You can't do that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, what was his name again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Monch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Monch. Oh, a different name. Okay. And Monch said, 'I will not stick my tongue on you. I only use my sticky tongue to catch bugs.' and then... he stuck his tongue out and grabbed a fly buzzing by in the air and then... and chomped it. 'I would like to teach you something, little moles, while you're in my swamp. Perhaps you could help me to find some herbs for my tea. Many herbs, which are little green plants with leaves that smell nice, grow in this swamp. I'm feeling like some mint tea. Do you know what mint looks like, little moles? Do you think you could find me some?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, my mint grew... my mint, I don't have any mint.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, but there's plenty of mint growing in the swamp these days. Look, follow me. And the lizard man stood up to his full height and gracefully stepped through the swamp and leaned over behind another tree and said, 'Aha! Here's some. Will you pick that for me, little mole?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did the moles pick the mint?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pick, pick, pick. Yes, two bunches would be just right. 'Here, bring it over to me and I'll put it in my tea kettle.' And the lizard man lit a little tiny fire and he poured some water in the kettle and heated it up. And soon enough, there was a delicious minty smell coming from the tea kettle and he said, 'Thank you for your help, little moles. I will pour us all some tea.' And he poured the tea into beautiful little cups. And it was hot, so he said, 'You must blow on it to cool it down. [Blowing sound]'. And then he said, 'Little moles, I will teach you one more thing. It is a skill called meditation, and I do a lot of it here in the swamp. Do you know about meditation?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, to meditate you must sit quietly and close your eyes and think of something wonderful. The first kind of meditation I will share with you is a love meditation. You sit quietly and think of someone that loves you. Who loves you, little moles?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Papa Mole. And so think of how it feels to have Papa Mole's love inside you, and breathe deeply in... and feel Papa Mole's love glowing and warm inside you and then breathe out... and then breathe in again... and then breathe out. And that's the love meditation.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm thinking of my whole lizard folk family. My wonderful parents and my brothers and sisters and my little lizard children, and I get such a wonderful warm glow inside me when I think of them when I do the love meditation. Would you like to do one more meditation?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh-huh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: This one is called the laughing meditation. We just do a little giggle. 'Hehe'. And then we laugh a little more. 'Haha'. It's a silly meditation. Hehe, haha.

characters.name: The Mischief Moles
characters.description: A troupe of adventurous little moles who attended Flutter’s birthday party in sturdy galoshes, fresh from exploring the forest’s hidden trails and mossy hollows.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Monch
characters.description: A friendly, person-sized lizard folk creature with green scales, yellow eyes, and a long sticky tongue who enjoys tea and meditation.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: The Mouse
characters.description: A small creature mentioned as being the one who eats all the nut snacks.

places.name: The Swamp
places.description: A wet, mucky part of the forest filled with ferns, moss, and trees growing out of the water.
